ucs.title: [LLM] Midnight Blizzard (APT29) password spray over legacy auth from residential-proxy infrastructure
ucs.description: Hunts the exact tradecraft Microsoft disclosed in the January 2024 Midnight Blizzard corporate-tenant breach: low-and-slow password spray against AAD legacy auth endpoints (`ClientAppUsed = 'Other clients'`) from distributed residential-proxy IPs, hitting many users with classic spray error codes before achieving a single quiet success. APT29 deliberately routes through residential ASNs to defeat IP-rep blocks and prefers legacy auth because it bypasses MFA enforcement on most CA policies.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true dc(Authentication.user) AS FailedUserCount values(Authentication.user) AS FailedUsers count AS FailedAttempts values(Authentication.app) AS TargetApps values(Authentication.src_category) AS SrcASNs FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE Authentication.action="failure" AND Authentication.signature_id IN (50053,50055,50056,50057,50064,50126,50133,50173) AND (Authentication.authentication_method="Other clients" OR Authentication.app="Other clients" OR Authentication.user_agent="BAV2ROPC") BY Authentication.src _time span=1h | where FailedUserCount>=10 | rename Authentication.* AS * | join type=left src [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Authentication.user) AS SuccessUsers FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE Authentication.action="success" earliest=-1d BY Authentication.src | rename Authentication.src AS src] | eval HasSuccess=if(isnotnull(SuccessUsers),"YES","no") | sort - FailedUserCount
ucs.kql:
let WindowMinutes = 60;
let MinFailedUsers = 10;
// AAD spray-typical error codes: bad password, locked, disabled, expired pwd, no such user
let SprayErrors = dynamic([50053, 50055, 50056, 50057, 50064, 50126, 50133, 50173, 50034]);
let Sprays =
    AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(1d)
    | where ClientAppUsed in ("Other clients", "Other Clients")     // legacy auth — APT29 hallmark
    | where ErrorCode in (SprayErrors)
    | summarize
        FailedUserCount = dcount(AccountUpn),
        FailedUsers     = make_set(AccountUpn, 50),
        FailedAttempts  = count(),
        TargetApps      = make_set(Application, 20),
        Countries       = make_set(Country, 10),
        UAStrings       = make_set(UserAgent, 10)
      by IPAddress, bin(Timestamp, WindowMinutes * 1m)
    | where FailedUserCount >= MinFailedUsers;       // 10 distinct users from one IP/hour
Sprays
| join kind=leftouter (
    AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(1d)
    | where ErrorCode == 0
    | summarize SuccessAccounts = make_set(AccountUpn, 50) by IPAddress
  ) on IPAddress
| extend SuccessAfterSpray = iif(isnotempty(SuccessAccounts), "YES", "no")
| project Timestamp, IPAddress, FailedUserCount, FailedAttempts, TargetApps, Countries, UAStrings, SuccessAfterSpray, SuccessAccounts
| order by SuccessAfterSpray desc, FailedUserCount desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let WindowMinutes = 60;
let MinFailedUsers = 10;
let SprayErrors = dynamic([50053, 50055, 50056, 50057, 50064, 50126, 50133, 50173, 50034]);
let LegacyFailures =
    union isfuzzy=true
        ( SigninLogs
            | where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
            | where ClientAppUsed in ("Other clients", "Other Clients")
            | where ResultType in (SprayErrors)
            | extend Country = tostring(parse_json(LocationDetails).countryOrRegion) ),
        ( AADNonInteractiveUserSignInLogs
            | where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
            | where ClientAppUsed in ("Other clients", "Other Clients")
            | where ResultType in (SprayErrors)
            | extend Country = tostring(parse_json(Location).countryOrRegion) );
let Sprays =
    LegacyFailures
    | summarize
        FailedUserCount = dcount(UserPrincipalName),
        FailedUsers     = make_set(UserPrincipalName, 50),
        FailedAttempts  = count(),
        TargetApps      = make_set(AppDisplayName, 20),
        Countries       = make_set(Country, 10),
        UAStrings       = make_set(UserAgent, 10),
        ASNs            = make_set(AutonomousSystemNumber, 10)
      by IPAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, WindowMinutes * 1m)
    | where FailedUserCount >= MinFailedUsers;
Sprays
| join kind=leftouter (
    SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(1d)
    | where ResultType == 0
    | summarize SuccessAccounts = make_set(UserPrincipalName, 50) by IPAddress
  ) on IPAddress
| extend SuccessAfterSpray = iif(isnotempty(SuccessAccounts), "YES", "no")
| project TimeGenerated, IPAddress, FailedUserCount, FailedAttempts, TargetApps, Countries, ASNs, UAStrings, SuccessAfterSpray, SuccessAccounts
| order by SuccessAfterSpray desc, FailedUserCount desc
ucs.rationale: Generic password-spray detections fire constantly on Exchange AutoDiscover and OWA brute-force. This rule narrows to (a) legacy-auth client app, (b) the specific AAD error codes Microsoft published as the Midnight Blizzard fingerprint, (c) >=10 distinct sprayed users per source per hour, and (d) flags any source that ALSO produced a successful auth — exactly the asymmetric pattern SVR uses to vali

ucs.title: [LLM] APT29 service-principal credential addition followed by SP sign-in from previously-unseen IP
ucs.description: Targets APT29/Midnight Blizzard's signature post-spray persistence move: adding a new client secret or certificate to an existing OAuth application / service principal (often a legacy test app with Graph Mail.ReadWrite or full_access_as_app), then immediately authenticating as that SP from attacker-controlled infrastructure to scrape mailboxes via Graph/EWS. Microsoft Threat Intelligence and Mandiant call this the 'application-actor' pattern (also seen in MagicWeb / 2023 Microsoft / HPE intrusions).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true earliest(_time) AS CredAddTime values(Change.user) AS Initiator values(Change.src) AS InitiatorIp values(Change.command) AS Operation FROM datamodel=Change WHERE (Change.action="created" OR Change.action="modified") AND Change.object_category="service_principal" AND (Change.command="Add service principal credentials." OR Change.command="Update application – Certificates and secrets management" OR Change.command="Update application") BY Change.object AS SPId | join type=inner SPId [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Authentication.src) AS SignInIPs values(Authentication.dest) AS Resources earliest(_time) AS SignInTime FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE Authentication.action="success" AND Authentication.user_type="ServicePrincipal" earliest=-7d BY Authentication.user AS SPId _time span=10m] | where SignInTime>=CredAddTime AND SignInTime<=relative_time(CredAddTime,"+24h") | join type=left SPId [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Authentication.src) AS BaselineIPs FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE Authentication.action="success" AND Authentication.user_type="ServicePrincipal" earliest=-30d latest=-7d BY Authentication.user AS SPId] | eval IsNewIP=if(isnull(mvfind(BaselineIPs,SignInIPs)),"YES","no") | where IsNewIP="YES" | table CredAddTime, SignInTime, SPId, Initiator, InitiatorIp, Operation, SignInIPs, Resources
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 7d;
// SP credential / application secret modifications surfaced via M365 audit
let CredAdds =
    CloudAppEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
    | where Application in ("Microsoft Azure", "Office 365")
    | where ActionType in~ (
        "Add service principal credentials.",
        "Update application – Certificates and secrets management.",
        "Update application.",
        "Add service principal.",
        "Update service principal.")
    | extend Target = tostring(ObjectName),
             SPId   = tostring(ObjectId)
    | project CredAddTime = Timestamp, Target, SPId,
              ActionType, Initiator = AccountDisplayName,
              InitiatorIp = IPAddress, InitiatorCountry = CountryCode,
              UA = UserAgent, RawEventData;
// SP-context sign-ins for those SPs in the 24h after the credential add
AADSignInEventsBeta
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where ErrorCode == 0
| where TokenIssuerType == "AzureAD"
| where isnotempty(ApplicationId)
| join kind=inner CredAdds on $left.ApplicationId == $right.SPId
| where Timestamp between (CredAddTime .. CredAddTime + 24h)
| extend SignInTime = Timestamp
// Anti-baseline: was this IP ever used by this SP in the 30d before?
| join kind=leftanti (
    AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp between (ago(30d) .. ago(lookback))
    | where ErrorCode == 0
    | summarize by ApplicationId, IPAddress
  ) on ApplicationId, IPAddress
| project SignInTime, CredAddTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', SignInTime, CredAddTime),
          Application, ApplicationId, IPAddress, Country, ResourceDisplayName,
          Initiator, InitiatorIp, InitiatorCountry, ActionType
| order by SignInTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let lookback = 7d;
let CredAdds =
    AuditLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
    | where OperationName in~ (
        "Add service principal credentials",
        "Update application \u2013 Certificates and secrets management",
        "Update application",
        "Add service principal",
        "Update service principal")
    | where Result == "success"
    | mv-expand TargetResources
    | extend SPName = tostring(TargetResources.displayName),
             SPId   = tostring(TargetResources.id),
             ModProps = tostring(TargetResources.modifiedProperties)
    | where ModProps has_any ("KeyDescription", "PasswordCredentials", "KeyCredentials")
    | extend Initiator   = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.userPrincipalName),
             InitiatorIp = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.ipAddress),
             InitiatorApp = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).app.displayName)
    | project CredAddTime = TimeGenerated, SPName, SPId, OperationName, Initiator, InitiatorIp, InitiatorApp;
let SPBaselineIPs =
    AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(30d) .. ago(lookback))
    | where ResultType == 0
    | summarize BaselineIPs = make_set(IPAddress, 500) by ServicePrincipalId;
AADServicePrincipalSignInLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where ResultType == 0
| join kind=inner CredAdds on $left.ServicePrincipalId == $right.SPId
| where TimeGenerated between (CredAddTime .. CredAddTime + 24h)
| join kind=leftouter SPBaselineIPs on ServicePrincipalId
| extend IsNewIP = iif(isnull(BaselineIPs) or array_index_of(BaselineIPs, IPAddress) < 0, true, false)
| where IsNewIP == true
| project SignInTime = TimeGenerated, CredAddTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', TimeGenerated, CredAddTime),
          ServicePrincipalName, ServicePrincipalId, IPAddress, Location,
          ResourceDisplayName, OperationName, Initiator, InitiatorIp, InitiatorApp
| order by SignInTime desc
ucs.rationale: Pure 'new SP credential' detections drown in legitimate DevOps secret-rotation noise. This rule fires only when (a) a credential is added to an existing SP, (b) that SP authenticates within 24h, AND (c) from an IP never seen for that SP in the prior 30 days — the precise sequence Microsoft documented for Midnight Blizzard's January 2024 corporate-tenant intrusion and the same chain Mandiant attrib
